title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a woman giving information about a workshop at a garden centre.
audio_script: Woman: Hello, everyone, and welcome to the garden centre. Today’s workshop is for people who want to make a small garden at home. We’ll look at several kinds of plants, but the main focus will be [14]roses[/14], because they grow well in this area and are easy to look after. We’ll start in the large [15]greenhouse[/15], where the plants are warmer and we can work even if it rains. Please take a [16]notebook[/16] with you, as I’ll give you the names of useful tools and websites. There will be a short break at [17]eleven fifteen[/17]. Tea and coffee are free, but if you want cake you’ll need to pay for it. Lunch today is a simple [18]salad[/18] made with vegetables from our own garden. If you prefer something hot, the café next door is open until two. For visitors who drove here, the [19]car park[/19] is behind the shop, not beside the main road. You can leave your car there all day without paying. After lunch, you’ll each plant something to take home. The workshop is suitable for complete beginners, so please ask questions if something is not clear. You will not need to buy anything today, although there will be a discount in the shop after the session. Children over twelve can join if an adult stays with them. At the end, we will give each person a small plant to take home, plus instructions for looking after it during the first month.  Please do not bring large bags into the greenhouse, as the paths between the tables are quite narrow.

questions.explanation: The speaker says the main focus will be roses.
The speaker says they will start in the large greenhouse.
The speaker asks people to take a notebook with them.
The speaker says there will be a short break at eleven fifteen.
The speaker says lunch is a simple salad.
The speaker says the car park is behind the shop and is free.
